## Checklist

<!-- Mark completed items with an "x" -->

### Code Quality
- [ ] My code follows the project's style guidelines
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have run `ruff check` and `black` for code formatting
- [ ] I have run `mypy` for type checking

### Testing
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes
- [ ] I have tested this on my local environment

### Documentation
- [ ] I have updated the documentation accordingly
- [ ] I have updated the CHANGELOG.md
- [ ] I have added docstrings to new functions/classes
- [ ] I have updated type hints

### Dependencies
- [ ] I have not added new dependencies
- [ ] OR: I have added new dependencies and updated `pyproject.toml` and `requirements.txt`
- [ ] OR: I have updated existing dependencies (explain why below)

### Security
- [ ] My changes do not introduce security vulnerabilities
- [ ] I have not exposed sensitive information (API keys, passwords, etc.)
- [ ] I have followed security best practices
